sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.20 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Suppose we have$[a,b]=[c,d]$in an absolutely free group, where$a, b, [a,b]$are basic commutators (in some fixed free generators). If$c$and$d$are arbitrary (proper) commutators, does it follow that$a=c$and$b=d$? { A.\,Gaglione, D.\,Spellman} No, not always. For example, if$x_1,\, x_2,\, x_3$are free generators, \$x_1<x_2< x_3$,$a=[[x_2,x_1],x_3]$, \$b=[x_2,x_1]$, \$c=b^{-1}$, \$d=b^{x_3b}$(V.\,G.\,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
